_comment: This file is OVERWRITTEN each security review. Do NOT append to it.
fixes_applied: CRITICAL: Added 5 missing security headers to Nginx (X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, X-XSS-Protection, Referrer-Policy, Content-Security-Policy). Headers were documented in security-guide.md but never implemented.
Added client_max_body_size 50M to main server block (was only set on /codex location)

ssh_attacks.fail2ban_status: active - sshd jail running, 7 currently banned, 2494 total banned
ssh_attacks.change_since_last: Total attempts up to 8809 (from 8560). fail2ban confirmed active with 2494 total bans. 213.209.159.159 now top recent attacker. Crypto brute force campaign continues.

pdf_security.innerHTML_note: 4 usages in upload.js/viewer.js - all static templates, no user data

cgi_security.note: CGI location block configured in nginx but no CGI scripts found in webroot cgi-bin directory

known_issues: No general *.md nginx block - only CLAUDE.md specifically blocked. tasks.md intentionally served for task viewer. Any new .md file added to web root would be publicly accessible.
UFW firewall is inactive - only SSH and HTTP/HTTPS ports are needed
disable_symlinks off in nginx config - low risk since only novakj can write to webroot
notes: Full security review completed - 20 checks passed
FIXED: Added 5 security headers to Nginx that were documented but never implemented
FIXED: Added client_max_body_size 50M to main server block
Previous recommendation to install fail2ban is now RESOLVED - it was already active (7 banned, 2494 total)
JS code review: all 12 JS files clean - no eval(), Function(), document.write(). innerHTML used only with static templates
PDF validation in utils.js implements all 4 checks: MIME type, extension, size limit (50MB), magic bytes (%PDF-)
SSL certificate valid until June 18, 2026 (~79 days remaining)
recommendations: Enable UFW firewall to restrict unnecessary ports (sudo ufw allow 22,80,443/tcp && sudo ufw enable)
Add general *.md deny rule to nginx with explicit exception for tasks.md (defense in depth)
Consider changing disable_symlinks to 'on' in nginx config for defense in depth
